fix: adaptive status polling — 3s when ingesting, 30s when idle

Commit 978d7eb merged status poll (was 2s) + stats poll (was 10s) into
a single 30s interval to reduce overhead. This caused the ingestion
progress badge in the header to not appear for up to 30 seconds after
ingestion started — essentially invisible.

Fix: adaptive interval. Initial fetch is immediate. When isBusy changes
false→true, reschedule at 3s. When isBusy changes true→false, reschedule
at 30s. This keeps the idle overhead low while restoring real-time
ingestion feedback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(types,client): add Message.chat, reply_to_message_id, compat methods

- Remove dead _lib/_ptr slots from Message (never set, caused confusion)
- Add _client slot: safe Python reference set by _c_handler, propagated to reply_to_message

- Add async Message.reply(), edit(), delete(), forward() Pyrogram-compat methods

- Add _MessageChat class

These fix TelegramContext.chat/is_reply AttributeError that caused all commands to fail silently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: illuminate uses compound_id not tags table for content pull

Root cause: tags table only contains tag stub rows (atom_id = tag_atom_id).
Content atom tags live in atoms.tags (JSON column), not the tags table.
Querying tags WHERE tag IN (...) JOIN atoms WHERE source != atom_source
returned 0 rows because every matching row IS a tag atom.

New approach: content atoms have compound_id pointing to their parent
mem_ compound. Query atoms WHERE compound_id IN (<top_hubs>) directly.

Changes:
- Replace fetchContentAtomsByTopTags with fetchContentAtomsByHubs(hubIds)
- Illuminate path skips edge-BFS entirely — goes straight hub → content
- Hub count = seed_count × 20 (default 200 hubs) capped at 500
- Atoms returned in hub-rank order then start_byte (document order)
- Budget trim still applies to limit final output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace auto cgroup edit with manual preflight check in install scripts

The sed-based auto-edit was causing boot failures. Scripts now check for
cgroup flags and exit with clear manual instructions if missing, rather
than attempting to modify cmdline.txt automatically.

README Quick Start updated to include the manual cmdline.txt step with
reboot before running any install scripts.

commit.message:
Phase 8.2: Azure Functions backend API for user profiles and scenarios (#71)

Implements the Azure Functions API layer (Phase 8.2) — Table Storage
services, auth middleware, CRUD endpoints for user profiles and
scenarios, ETag-based conflict detection, unit tests, and API
documentation.

## New API Endpoints

| Method | Route | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| GET/PUT | `/api/user/profile` | User profile (auto-provisions on first
GET) |
| GET/POST | `/api/scenarios` | List / create scenarios |
| GET/PUT/DELETE | `/api/scenarios/{id}` | Single scenario operations |
| POST | `/api/scenarios/{id}/activate` | Set active scenario + syncs
profile |

## Service Layer (`api/src/services/`)

- **`table-storage.js`** — `TableStorageService` wrapping
`@azure/data-tables` with `createEntity / getEntity / updateEntity /
deleteEntity / listEntities` (OData filter + cursor pagination). Factory
reads `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING`.
- **`user-profile.js`** — Profile CRUD; auto-creates with defaults
(`theme: light`, `defaultPeriod: fortnightly`) on first access.
- **`scenarios.js`** — Scenario CRUD with name validation and ETag-based
optimistic concurrency:

```js
// updateScenario throws { conflict: true, serverVersion } when ETags mismatch
// Function layer translates this to HTTP 409 with serverVersion in body
if (updates.etag && updates.etag !== '*' && updates.etag !== existing.etag) {
    const err = new Error('Conflict');
    err.conflict = true;
    err.serverVersion = this._toScenario(existing);
    throw err;
}
```

## Auth Middleware (`api/src/utils/auth.js`)

Decodes the `x-ms-client-principal` base64 header injected by SWA.
`requireAuth()` throws a structured `{ status: 401 }` error consumed
uniformly by all function handlers.

## Dependencies

- `@azure/data-tables@^13.3.2` added to `api/package.json` (no known
CVEs)
- `jest@^30` added as dev dependency; `npm test` in `api/` runs 36 new
unit tests

## Tests

36 new unit tests across `auth.test.js`, `table-storage.test.js`, and
`scenarios.test.js` — covering CRUD, error paths, validation, and ETag
conflict detection. All 316 tests (280 pre-existing + 36 new) pass.

## Documentation

`documentation/api-reference.md` — full endpoint reference with
request/response shapes, error codes, environment variables, and cURL
examples.

feat: per-tenant full palette UI theming with party defaults + inheritance

## Theme engine (src/lib/tenant-theme.ts)
- TenantTheme type: primaryColor, secondaryColor, accentColor,
  sidebarFrom, sidebarTo, topbarBg, logoUrl, faviconUrl
- PARTY_THEMES: party-appropriate defaults for all 7 affiliations
  (Republican=red, Democrat=blue, Libertarian=gold, Green=green, etc.)
- mergeTheme(), themeFromBranding(), buildCssVars() helpers

## Effective theme API (GET /api/tenant/[orgId]/effective-theme)
- Walks parentId ancestry up to 5 levels
- Layers: DEFAULT → party theme → ancestors → own branding
- Returns { theme, inheritedFrom } — inheritedFrom shows parent org name
  when any field is inherited rather than set directly

## Settings API
- PATCH /settings now accepts: secondaryColor, accentColor,
  sidebarFrom, sidebarTo, topbarBg
- GET /settings returns all new fields

## TenantThemeProvider
- Client component fetching effective theme, injecting CSS vars
  (--t-primary, --t-secondary, --t-accent, --t-sidebar-from,
   --t-sidebar-to, --t-topbar-bg) onto :root
- Cleans up on unmount to prevent theme bleed between orgs

## Portal shell updated to CSS vars
- TenantNavigation: sidebar gradient via linear-gradient(var(--t-sidebar-from), var(--t-sidebar-to))
  active nav link via backgroundColor: var(--t-primary)
  inactive hover via bg-white/10 (works on any dark sidebar)
- TenantTopBar: topbar bg via var(--t-topbar-bg)
  title gradient via from-[var(--t-primary)] to-[var(--t-secondary)]

## Branding settings page rebuilt
- Full 6-color palette pickers + logo/favicon
- Live portal preview panel showing colors in real time
- Inherited badges showing which fields come from parent tenant
- Reset to party defaults button
- Saves all theme fields via existing updateSettings hook

## Seed
- 7 master tenant orgs in prod seeded with party-appropriate branding

fix: exclude tag stubs from illuminate content pull via atoms JOIN

Tag atoms (source_path='atom_source') self-tag with their own label,
so they appear in the tags table and were being returned as results.

The JS filter !id.startsWith('atom_source') was wrong — that checks
the ID prefix, not the source_path field. All atom IDs start with 'atom_'.

Fix: JOIN atoms ON a.source_path != 'atom_source' in the tags query,
filtering stubs at the SQL level before they enter the score map.

  scan for the same item (O(n) → O(n/2) average)

  variable capture with HashMap lookup (O(n+m) total)

commit.message:
Adaptive FPS camera: 320x240, starts at 8fps, auto-tunes 2-15fps

- Reduced resolution to 320x240 and JPEG quality to 18 for tiny frames
- Starts at 8 FPS, measures push duration and drop rate every 30 frames
- Scales down if drops > 30% or push time > 80% of interval
- Scales up if drops < 5% and push time < 40% of interval
- Uses recursive setTimeout so interval adapts in real-time

fix: add characterAssetsLoaded handler to render PNG sprite sheets

The webview had no handler for the characterAssetsLoaded message type
sent by the extension host. This caused the characterSheets Map to
never be populated, so drawCharacterFromSheet() always returned false
and all characters fell back to inline colored-rectangle sprites.

  via Image(), strips baked backgrounds with removeBackground(), and
  computes frame dimensions matching the existing loadAssets() logic
- useExtensionMessages.ts: Add characterAssetsLoaded case that calls

feat: add proactive-watchers skill - URL/API monitoring with AI triggers

Multi-runtime skill (Claude/Copilot/Gemini) that polls URLs and APIs
at configurable intervals and triggers AI agent prompts when conditions
are met.

Core features:
- 7 condition types: value_changed, new_item, status_code_change,
  text_contains, value_exceeds, regex_match, content_hash_changed
- 4 trigger methods: background_task, shell, webhook, log_only
- State persistence with JSONL event history
- Exponential backoff on errors
- Concurrent watchers via thread pool
- Template variable substitution in prompts
- Zero external dependencies (stdlib + curl)

Includes 4 example templates: github_releases, price_monitor,
website_status, api_monitor.

feat(image-to-3d): add TripoSR CPU backend for true 3D reconstruction

Previously, when no GPU or cloud API keys were available, the skill
fell back to depth-anything which produced flat 2D depth reliefs
instead of true 3D models (Z/X ratio ~5.8%).

Changes:
- Add triposr_cpu backend that runs TripoSR directly on CPU
- Add PyMCubes-based torchmcubes shim (CUDA not required)
- Update converter backend selection: GPU → cloud → CPU TripoSR
- Update fallback chain to include triposr_cpu

Results with Snorty pig image:
- True volumetric mesh: 73K vertices, 147K faces
- Watertight geometry, Euler number 2
- Z/X ratio: 77% (vs 5.8% with depth-anything)
- Vertex colors preserved
- Exports to GLB/OBJ/STL successfully
